page: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-400s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 2 shared vCPUs mapped to 1 physical AMD EPYC core. Operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, the processor features a 16 MB L3 cache and supports AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instruction sets. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, featuring a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities due to the shared CPU allocation. The stress-ng single-core CPU test registers a strong performance of 2934.07, placing it in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance drops to a weak rating in the bottom 25% with a stress-ng score of 5824.37 and a PassMark CPU score of 5470.65. Memory bandwidth is average for large blocks, reaching 74.28 GB/sec for read and 53.91 GB/sec for write operations, though small-block cached reads are weak at 296.86 GB/sec.

Given the shared CPU architecture and weak multi-core scaling, this server is not suited for compute-heavy parallel processing or high-throughput web serving, as evidenced by a poor static web serving throughput of 2.26 GB/sec. However, the generous 16.0 GB memory capacity and 400 GB NVMe SSD storage make it a cost-efficient option for memory-intensive but CPU-light workloads. It is appropriate for lightweight database hosting, caching layers, and basic text processing or small-scale LLM inference, where memory capacity and local storage space are prioritized over sustained multi-core CPU performance.
description: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-400s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 2 shared vCPUs on a 2.0 GHz AMD EPYC processor with 16.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it delivers strong single-core CPU performance, scoring 2934.07 in stress-ng benchmarks, but exhibits weak multi-core performance and database operations due to its shared CPU allocation. Memory bandwidth is moderate, with large-block reads averaging 74.28 GB/sec. Lacking a GPU, this server is not designed for heavy parallel computing or high-throughput web serving, which is reflected in its low static web serving throughput of 2.26 GB/sec. Instead, its high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core and substantial local NVMe storage make it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for memory-optimized, CPU-light workloads such as lightweight databases, caching, and basic text processing.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-400s-amd is a memory-optimized server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core but weak multi-core performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized shared instance pairing 16 GB RAM with 400 GB NVMe storage for lightweight, memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs with 16.0 GB system memory
Bundled 400 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance but weak multi-core scaling
High memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core
Best suited for lightweight databases, caching, and memory-intensive, CPU-light tasks
